data.mppt1_voltage_sensor: MPPT 1 DC voltage sensor (optional, for curtailment detection)

data_description.pv_actual_sensor: Best practice: a cumulative energy counter (Wh/kWh, total_increasing). If you only have a power reading, use a rolling mean_linear statistics helper (kW) — not a raw instantaneous sensor. Whole-system total — multi-array systems also map each array on the per-site page that follows.
data_description.pv_export_sensor: Cumulative export energy counter (Wh/kWh) preferred; otherwise a rolling mean_linear helper (kW).
data_description.pv_actual_input_mode: Auto-detect picks energy counter vs averaged power from the unit (Wh/kWh vs W/kW).
data_description.pv_export_input_mode: Auto-detect by unit. Energy counter recommended.
data_description.mppt1_voltage_sensor: Optional per-MPPT DC string sensors (e.g. from Fronius). Point at the raw per-string voltage/current entities — the integration takes the maximum voltage and minimum current over each 30-minute slot from history, so no statistics helper is needed. Voltage climbing toward open-circuit while current collapses is the off-MPP fingerprint of export curtailment. MPPT 2 is optional; single-tracker inverters leave it blank. Shown only for single-array systems — multi-array systems map each array's MPPT voltage/current in the per-site step instead.

database.title: Storage
database.description: History is stored in a built-in file (config/solcast_solar_enhanced.db) — no setup required. Leave enabled for tuning and dampening to work.

data_description.db_retention_days: 0 keeps all history (default). A value bounds the database on low-power devices by deleting older rows daily — but seasonal dampening uses a cross-year window, so keep at least ~400 days (≈13 months) if you rely on it.

data_description.openmeteo_enabled: Free, keyless source of solar irradiance (required for PV tilt tuning) plus cloud cover and temperature. Leave enabled unless you specifically prefer OpenWeatherMap.
data_description.owm_enabled: Optional legacy alternative for cloud cover and temperature; needs a free API key. When enabled it takes precedence over Open-Meteo for cloud/temperature — irradiance still comes from Open-Meteo.

data.dampening_gate: Warn when tuning disagrees with Solcast orientation

sites.title: Per-site sensor mapping
sites.description: {count} Solcast sites detected. First choose how your arrays are measured. 'Each array has its own generation sensor' — assign each array its own AC sensor (microinverters, e.g. Enphase, or one inverter per array); leave DC blank. 'One shared inverter' — put the same whole-system AC sensor on every array and give each its DC/MPPT sensor so output is split by DC share. Optionally add each array's MPPT voltage/current for diagnostics. Leave a site blank to skip it. 'Sensor type' is usually Auto-detect.

error.dc_split_missing_dc: DC-split needs a DC/MPPT sensor for every array. Add the missing DC sensor, or switch to 'each array has its own generation sensor'.
error.dc_split_ac_mismatch: DC-split needs the same shared AC generation sensor on every array. Use one identical AC sensor on all rows, or switch to 'each array has its own generation sensor'.

data.mppt1_voltage_sensor: MPPT 1 DC voltage sensor (optional, for curtailment detection)

data_description.pv_export_sensor: Cumulative export energy counter (Wh/kWh) preferred; otherwise a rolling mean_linear helper (kW).
data_description.pv_actual_input_mode: Auto-detect picks energy counter vs averaged power from the unit (Wh/kWh vs W/kW).
data_description.pv_export_input_mode: Auto-detect by unit. Energy counter recommended.
data_description.mppt1_voltage_sensor: Optional per-MPPT DC string sensors (e.g. from Fronius). Point at the raw per-string voltage/current entities — the integration takes the maximum voltage and minimum current over each 30-minute slot from history, so no statistics helper is needed. Voltage climbing toward open-circuit while current collapses is the off-MPP fingerprint of export curtailment. MPPT 2 is optional; single-tracker inverters leave it blank.

data.dampening_gate: Warn when tuning disagrees with Solcast orientation

sites.title: Per-site sensor mapping
sites.description: {count} Solcast sites detected. First choose how your arrays are measured. 'Each array has its own generation sensor' — assign each array its own AC sensor (microinverters, e.g. Enphase, or one inverter per array); leave DC blank. 'One shared inverter' — put the same whole-system AC sensor on every array and give each its DC/MPPT sensor so output is split by DC share. Optionally add each array's MPPT voltage/current for diagnostics. Leave a site blank to skip it. 'Sensor type' is usually Auto-detect.

error.dc_split_missing_dc: DC-split needs a DC/MPPT sensor for every array. Add the missing DC sensor, or switch to 'each array has its own generation sensor'.
error.dc_split_ac_mismatch: DC-split needs the same shared AC generation sensor on every array. Use one identical AC sensor on all rows, or switch to 'each array has its own generation sensor'.

options.direct: Each array has its own generation sensor (microinverters / one inverter per array)
options.dc_split: One shared inverter, split across arrays by DC (single inverter, multiple MPPTs)

owm_required.title: No weather source for PV tuning & dampening
owm_required.description: PV tuning and adaptive dampening need cloud cover (to find clear-sky periods) and, for tuning, solar irradiance. These normally come from Open-Meteo, which is free and needs no API key — but it has been disabled and no OpenWeatherMap key is configured, so every record's weather is unknown and excluded, leaving these features inactive (dampening stays neutral and is not pushed to Solcast). Re-enable Open-Meteo (or add an OpenWeatherMap API key) in this integration's options, or ignore this if you only want history logging.

orientation_diverged.title: Tuned tilt differs from your Solcast site
orientation_diverged.description: PV tuning has settled on a panel tilt that differs materially from the tilt your Solcast site is configured with. This is a heads-up only — adaptive dampening is still being calculated and pushed as normal. Before changing anything in Solcast, check the Tuning RMSE sensor for that array: tilt is often poorly determined from a short or heavily shaded history, and a low-confidence fit can be tens of degrees out without meaning anything is wrong. If the fit is good and the difference persists across several days, update your Solcast site tilt to match the Tuned Tilt value. (Azimuth is never tuned, so it is never the cause.) To stop this warning, turn off 'Warn when tuning disagrees with Solcast orientation' in the integration options.

granular_dampening_conflict.title: Solcast granular dampening conflicts with per-site dampening
granular_dampening_conflict.description:
The Solcast PV Forecast integration stores per-site dampening in a granular dampening file, and its current contents stop this integration's per-site factors from working.

Either the file contains an 'all' entry — which the base integration applies in preference to every per-site entry, so the factors pushed for your individual arrays are ignored — or its sites do not all use the same number of factors, which makes the base discard the whole file and disable dampening for every site. In the second case this integration stops pushing rather than trigger that. Check Home Assistant's log for which one applies.

To fix: edit solcast-dampening.json in your Home Assistant config directory so that it contains one entry per Solcast site, each with the same number of factors, and no 'all' entry. An 'all' entry is created by calling the Solcast 'set_dampening' action with 48 factors and no site. Clearing 'granular dampening' in the Solcast PV Forecast options deletes the file entirely, after which this integration rebuilds it on its next dampening run.
